rationale_for_baseline_tier: Bleeding excoriated lesions with behavioural crisis need prompt same-day in-person wound care and assessment, but no systemic infection or emergency — urgent primary/urgent care (T3).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever >38°C, spreading warmth/redness, skin hot to touch, honey-coloured crusting or pus
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek emergency/same-day medical review; ED if systemically unwell
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates secondary bacterial infection/cellulitis needing antibiotics and possibly admission.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If a fever develops, the redness spreads, the skin gets hot, or you see golden crusts or pus around the sores — get urgent medical review or go to ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Agitation and self-excoriation that support staff cannot safely manage
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek urgent help / ED if unsafe
escalation_edges.rationale: Escalating self-harm to the skin risks severe trauma; may need in-person crisis support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the picking and distress can't be safely managed by staff, seek urgent help or go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Intellectual disability — history and care mediated by support workers
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use plain language, direct instructions to support staff, and confirm understanding; document a clear staff wound-care and monitoring plan.
Support staff with clear monitoring criteria and escalation thresholds.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Arrange same-day in-person review for wound care. Use hydrocolloid patches and night-time cotton gloves, give the anti-itch medicine, and have staff clean the areas with saline. Watch for the infection signs below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP/urgent care; call 000 or attend ED if emergency signs develop
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use after-hours GP/urgent care; ED for fever, spreading infection, or unsafe agitation.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the support worker with a written wound-care and monitoring plan and the 3–5 day review arrangement.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care only advice with no infection safety-net or support-staff plan.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Open lesions superinfect unnoticed; the patient develops cellulitis/sepsis before review.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine (non-same-day) handling delays wound care and lets the itch-scratch cycle continue.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency-ambulance escalation for uncomplicated excoriated acne without infection or unsafe agitation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot inspect wounds closely or reliably exclude early superinfection/eczema herpeticum remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Screen for infection red flags, arrange prompt in-person wound care, and give a clear staff safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismissing as behavioural with no medical review, or over-escalating to emergency services without red flags.
